title: Enveloping Uruguayan Beef Stew
description: A malty, silky stew with a comforting deep flavor, highlighted by cider vinegar and slow‑cooked beef.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium‑high heat until shimmering.
Add beef cubes and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes, then remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium, add diced onion, carrots, and minced garlic; sauté until onion soft, about 8 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes, letting it caramelize slightly.
Pour in red wine, scraping any browned bits from the pot, and let simmer until the wine reduces by half, about 6 minutes.
Return the beef to the pot, add beef stock, cider vinegar, honey, and a bay leaf; bring to a gentle boil then reduce heat to low.
Cover and let the stew simmer for 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beef is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Season with salt and pepper, remove the bay leaf, stir in chopped parsley, and serve hot.
